# NOTICE — about the "Source code" archives in this repo

Every GitHub release page automatically shows a **Source code (zip)** and
**Source code (tar.gz)** download. Those archives are generated by GitHub
itself; they cannot be removed via the API.

The auto-attached archives **package this repository's contents at the
release tag** — and **this repository's contents are only**:

- `README.md` — public download instructions
- `NOTICE.md` — this file

So the auto-generated "Source code" archive contains exactly those two
documentation files. **No application source code is in this repository,
in any tag, in any release archive, or in any commit history.**

The full J2G application source is **closed-source** and lives in a
private repository that is never published to GitHub or any other
public surface.

## Where the actual binaries are

This repository's purpose is to host the **release artifacts** (DMG,
EXE, ZIP, AppImage, deb, signatures, checksums, public key). They are
attached as **release assets** to each [Release](../../releases) — not
as files in the repo tree, and not in the auto-generated source
archives.

## License

The compiled binary artifacts published in the Releases tab are
licensed under the J2G End-User License Agreement bundled with each
build. This repository's two documentation files are public.
